insight: Electronics is the leading revenue category
evidence: Electronics generated $156905, representing 34.4% of total revenue.
business_meaning: The business has a clear top category by revenue, making Electronics the main commercial revenue driver.
recommended_action: Prioritise Electronics in revenue-focused campaigns, homepage placements, and stock availability planning.

insight: Category roles are clearly different across revenue, volume, and premium value
evidence: Electronics leads revenue, Clothing leads units sold, and Beauty has the highest average order value.
business_meaning: The categories play different business roles: one leads revenue, one leads purchase volume, and one attracts higher-value baskets.
recommended_action: Use different strategies by category: protect Electronics revenue, use Clothing for volume-led promotions, and position Beauty as a premium basket-value opportunity.

insight: Sales performance is driven more by volume than by basket size
evidence: The strongest-to-weakest month comparison identifies Units sold as the main performance driver.
business_meaning: Revenue changes appear to depend more on the number of purchases and units sold than on customers spending more per order.
recommended_action: Focus sales initiatives on increasing traffic, conversion, and units per order rather than relying only on higher prices.

insight: May 2023 is the strongest sales month
evidence: 2023-05 generated $53150 from 105 transactions and 259 units sold.
business_meaning: May 2023 is the strongest observed sales period and can be used as a benchmark for strong monthly performance.
recommended_action: Review what could explain May performance and use it as a planning reference for promotions, inventory, and campaign timing.

insight: January 2024 should be treated as a partial-month performance caveat
business_meaning: January 2024 has very low transaction volume, so it should not be interpreted as a normal weak month without noting the partial-period issue.
recommended_action: Flag January 2024 as a partial period in dashboards and avoid using it as a direct benchmark against complete months.

insight: Female customers contribute slightly more revenue than male customers
business_meaning: Female customers are the slightly higher-value gender segment in the available KPI outputs.
recommended_action: Use gender-level revenue differences to inform customer messaging, while avoiding over-targeting until category preference tables are reviewed.

insight: The 45-54 age group is the highest revenue age segment
evidence: The 45-54 age group generated $97235 in revenue.
business_meaning: The 45-54 segment is the strongest age-based revenue contributor and should be considered a priority customer group.
recommended_action: Build age-group targeting and campaign messaging around the 45-54 segment while comparing its product preferences before final recommendations.
